address: 240 Mercer Street (Mercer Arts Center, in the former Broadway Central Hotel), Greenwich Village, NY
owner_or_host: Mercer Arts Center (host venue) / Electronic Arts Intermix (fiscal sponsor)
notes: The original Kitchen — a 30-foot-wide disused kitchen on the second floor; equipped with videotape machines, tape recorders, monitors, loudspeakers, a piano. Wednesday open-house screenings + weekly performances. Building (formerly Broadway Central Hotel) collapsed August 3, 1973.

address: 484 Broome Street (corner of Wooster and Broome), 2nd floor, SoHo, NY
source: Reopened November 1973 as a freestanding nonprofit (Haleakala Inc DBA The Kitchen). Some sources cite the corner address as 59 Wooster Street. Became 'New York's premiere avant-garde and experimental arts center.' Music + Dance + Video + Film + Literature programming all expanded here.

owner_or_host: Haleakala Inc (purchased from Dia Art Foundation in 1987)
notes: 1920s-era former icehouse. Inaugural Chelsea programming series 'New Ice Nights' (1986). Building purchased from Dia 1987. Black-box performance space + gallery + offices. Hurricane Sandy damage Oct 2012 (~$450K). Building closed for renovation Fall 2022.

address: 163B Bank Street (Westbeth Artists Housing), West Village, NY
notes: Satellite home during Without Walls programming period; The Kitchen presents performances and programs both at Westbeth and at partner venues across the city.
Programming distributed across NYC partner spaces during the renovation period; complete partner list not consolidated in publicly extractable form.
Architect Rice+Lipka Architects (Lyn Rice). Stated goals: maintain industrial character; double capacity; cross-disciplinary flexibility. Public reopening date not fixed as of May 2026.
